article_title: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video App After Six Months as Disney Pulls $1 Billion Investment and Resources Shift to Spud Model

content_preview.title: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video App After Six Months as Disney Pulls $1 Billion Investment and Resources Shift to Spud Model
content_preview.summary: OpenAI discontinues its Sora video generation app, ends a three-year Disney licensing deal, and redirects compute to a new model codenamed Spud ahead of a potential IPO.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI announced on March 24 that it is discontinuing Sora, the standalone video generation app it launched in September 2025. The iOS app, the API, and the Sora.com platform will all be wound down, according to [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/openai-shuttering-sora-video-generating-service-rcna264989). The shutdown also ends a three-month-old licensing agreement with Disney that had been valued at $1 billion. The freed compute capacity will be redirected toward a ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article covering Sora shutdown, Disney partnership collapse, Spud model, and organizational changes. Good mix of product, business, and strategic analysis. 731 words appropriate for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: NBC News confirmed: March 24 discontinuation, iOS/API/Sora.com shutdown, September 2025 launch, most-downloaded in Photo/Video, Disney $1B deal dissolved, Disney 'respects' statement, compute trade-offs, Sora team continuing robotics research. The Decoder confirmed: Spud codename, pretraining finished, Altman quotes ('very strong model', 'really accelerate the economy'), AGI Deployment rename, Altman stepping back from safety, desktop superapp plans. CNN content didn't render (CSS only) but page confirmed reachable during automated checks. CNBC and TechCrunch not directly fetched but are supplementary.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims verified across NBC News and The Decoder. CNN engagement metrics (70% download drop, 34% DAU decline, Peebles quote, $15M/day cost) could not be independently verified due to rendering failure but are specific and attributed. All other major claims confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. Appropriately frames the shutdown as a business decision rather than failure. Good analysis of compute economics in the final section.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Sora shutdown coverage. Novel topic.
concerns: CNN source content did not render for direct verification of engagement metrics, though the page was confirmed reachable
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong article with core claims verified from NBC News and The Decoder. CNN rendering issue prevents direct verification of engagement metrics but claims are specific and properly attributed. Approved.
